questions.question: Which number is divisible by $2$ and $5$?
What is the remainder when $17$ is divided by $5$?
A stock trader has $48$ shares to sell. If she wants to sell them in boxes of $4$, how many boxes can she make?
Which of the following numbers is NOT divisible by $3$?
What is the result of $24 div 3$?
A savings account has $150$. If $30$ is withdrawn, what is the remaining balance?
What is the remainder when $43$ is divided by $9$?
A tax consultant has $96$ files to organize. If she organizes them in folders of $8$, how many folders can she make?
Perform the long division: $63 div 5$. Express your result with a remainder.
A company has $240$ shares to distribute among its employees. If each employee receives $8$ shares, how many employees will receive shares? Show your work and express your result with a remainder.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of divisibility before attempting long division.
Tip 2: Provide examples of real-world applications of division in business and finance.
Tip 3: Encourage students to check their work by multiplying the quotient and divisor and adding the remainder.
